title: Left outer non-equi join throws `INVALID_JOIN_ON_EXPRESSION`
description:
### Company or project name
ClickHouse
### Question
In ClickHouse `25.10`
```sql
create table a (x UInt64) engine = MergeTree order by x;
create table b (y UInt64) engine = MergeTree order by y;
insert into a select * from generate_series(1,5);
insert into b select * from generate_series(3,6);
select a.x, b.y
from a left outer join b on a.x > b.y
```
throws
```
Received exception from server (version 25.9.3):
Code: 403. DB::Exception: Received from localhost:9000. DB::Exception: Cannot determine join keys in JOIN ON expression greater(__table1.x, __table2.y). (INVALID_JOIN_ON_EXPRESSION)
(query: select a.x, b.y
from a left outer join b on a.x > b.y)
```
Looking at the [docs](https://clickhouse.com/docs/sql-reference/statements/select/join#join-with-inequality-conditions-for-columns-from-different-tables), this non-equi join should theoretically work.
Interestingly, using a full outer join instead: `from a outer join b on a.x > b.y` makes it work.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE c (a BIGINT, b BIGINT) ENGINE = InnoDB;

INSERT INTO c SELECT number, CASE WHEN number % 2 = 0 THEN number ELSE NULL END FROM (SELECT 0 AS number UNION ALL SELECT 1 UNION ALL SELECT 2 UNION ALL SELECT 3 UNION ALL SELECT 4 UNION ALL SELECT 5 UNION ALL SELECT 6 UNION ALL SELECT 7 UNION ALL SELECT 8 UNION ALL SELECT 9);
CREATE TABLE d (m BIGINT, n BIGINT) ENGINE = InnoDB;
INSERT INTO d SELECT number, number + 1 FROM (SELECT 0 AS number UNION ALL SELECT 1 UNION ALL SELECT 2 UNION ALL SELECT 3 UNION ALL SELECT 4);
SELECT c.a, d.n FROM c LEFT JOIN d ON c.a > d.m;
key_bug_features: Left outer join with non-equi condition fails
